data.name: PressCtrlCAgainToForceExit
data.value: Press Ctrl+C again to force exit.
data.comment: {Locked="Ctrl+C"}

data.name: ForTest
data.comment: is followed by test name

data.name: MinimumExpectedTestsPolicyViolation
data.value: Minimum expected tests policy violation, tests ran {0}, minimum expected {1}
data.comment: {0} is the number of tests that ran. {1} is the minimum expected number of tests.

data.name: OutOfProcessArtifactsProduced
data.value: Out of process file artifacts produced:

data.name: ConsoleIsAlreadyInBatchingMode
data.value: Console is already in batching mode.
data.comment: Exception that is thrown when console is already collecting input into a batch (into a string builder), and code asks to enable batching mode again.

data.name: StackFrameAt
data.comment: at that is used for a stack frame location in a stack trace, is followed by a class and method name

data.name: StackFrameIn
data.comment: in that is used in stack frame it is followed by file name

data.name: TerminalNoAnsiOptionDescription
data.value: Disable outputting ANSI escape characters to screen.

data.name: TerminalAnsiOptionDescription
data.value: Control whether ANSI escape characters are emitted. Valid values are 'auto' (default), 'on' (also accepts 'true', 'enable', '1') or 'off' (also accepts 'false', 'disable', '0'). 'on' forces ANSI escape codes (including cursor movement) even when stdout is redirected; pair it with --progress off if you only want colors. When both --ansi and --no-ansi are provided, --ansi wins.
data.comment: {Locked="'auto'"}{Locked="'on'"}{Locked="'true'"}{Locked="'enable'"}{Locked="'1'"}{Locked="'off'"}{Locked="'false'"}{Locked="'disable'"}{Locked="'0'"}{Locked="--progress off"}{Locked="--ansi"}{Locked="--no-ansi"}

data.name: TerminalAnsiOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: --ansi expects a single parameter with value 'auto', 'on' (or 'true', 'enable', '1') or 'off' (or 'false', 'disable', '0').
data.comment: {Locked="--ansi"}{Locked="auto"}{Locked="on"}{Locked="true"}{Locked="enable"}{Locked="1"}{Locked="off"}{Locked="false"}{Locked="disable"}{Locked="0"}

data.name: TerminalNoProgressOptionDescription
data.value: [Deprecated, use '--progress off' instead] Disable reporting progress to screen.
data.comment: {Locked="--progress off"}

data.name: TerminalProgressOptionDescription
data.value: Control whether progress is reported to screen. Valid values are 'auto' (default), 'on' (also accepts 'true', 'enable', '1') or 'off' (also accepts 'false', 'disable', '0'). 'auto' shows progress unless the terminal cannot update in place (for example with --no-ansi or in CI). This option takes precedence over the deprecated --no-progress flag.
data.comment: {Locked="auto"}{Locked="on"}{Locked="true"}{Locked="enable"}{Locked="1"}{Locked="off"}{Locked="false"}{Locked="disable"}{Locked="0"}{Locked="--no-ansi"}{Locked="--no-progress"}

data.name: TerminalProgressOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: --progress expects a single parameter with value 'auto', 'on' (or 'true', 'enable', '1') or 'off' (or 'false', 'disable', '0').
data.comment: {Locked="--progress"}{Locked="auto"}{Locked="on"}{Locked="true"}{Locked="enable"}{Locked="1"}{Locked="off"}{Locked="false"}{Locked="disable"}{Locked="0"}

data.name: TerminalProgressHeartbeat
data.comment: Heartbeat line emitted on non-cursor terminals when the run goes silent. {0} is the number of completed tests, {1} is the number of failed tests.

data.name: TerminalProgressHeartbeatActiveSuffix
data.comment: Suffix appended to the heartbeat line naming the currently active test. {0} is the active test name.

data.name: TerminalProgressSlowTest
data.comment: Line emitted on non-cursor terminals when a single test runs longer than the slow-test threshold. {0} is the elapsed duration, {1} is the test description. {Locked="[slow]"}

data.name: TerminalOutputOptionDescription
data.value: Output verbosity when reporting tests. Valid values are 'Normal', 'Detailed'. Default is 'Normal'.
data.comment: {Locked="Normal"}{Locked="Detailed"}

data.name: TerminalOutputOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: --output expects a single parameter with value 'Normal' or 'Detailed'.
data.comment: {Locked="--output"}{Locked="Normal"}{Locked="Detailed"}

data.name: TerminalShowStdoutOptionDescription
data.value: Determines when to show captured standard output of a test. Valid values are 'All', 'Failed', 'None'. Default is 'All' (or 'Failed' when an LLM/AI agent environment is detected).
data.comment: {Locked="All"}{Locked="Failed"}{Locked="None"}

data.name: TerminalShowStderrOptionDescription
data.value: Determines when to show captured error output of a test. Valid values are 'All', 'Failed', 'None'. Default is 'All' (or 'Failed' when an LLM/AI agent environment is detected).
data.comment: {Locked="All"}{Locked="Failed"}{Locked="None"}

data.name: TerminalShowOutputOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: --show-stdout and --show-stderr expect a single parameter with value 'All', 'Failed', or 'None'.
data.comment: {Locked="--show-stdout"}{Locked="--show-stderr"}{Locked="All"}{Locked="Failed"}{Locked="None"}

data.name: TerminalShowSlowestTestsOptionDescription
data.value: Show the specified number of slowest tests (by reported execution duration) in the run summary. Expects a positive integer.

data.name: TerminalShowSlowestTestsOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: --show-slowest-tests expects a single positive integer parameter.
data.comment: {Locked="--show-slowest-tests"}

data.name: TestDiscoverySummarySingular
data.comment: {0} is the number of discovered tests.

data.name: ActiveTestsRunning_MoreTestsCount
data.comment: {0} is the number of additional active tests.

data.name: ActiveTestsRunning_FullTestsCount
data.comment: {0} is the number of currently running tests.

data.name: FailedWithErrors
data.comment: {0} is the number of errors in an assembly summary line

data.name: Try
data.comment: number of tries of the current test assembly when test assembly is being retried. {0} is number that starts at 1

data.name: Retried
data.comment: Suffix appended to the total test count in the run summary to indicate how many failed tests were retried, e.g. 'total: 5 (+2 retried)'.

data.name: RunningTestsFrom
data.comment: Per-assembly banner printed by the dotnet test orchestrator before running an assembly; followed by the assembly path/target framework/architecture.

data.name: DiscoveringTestsFrom
data.comment: Per-assembly banner printed by the dotnet test orchestrator before discovering tests in an assembly; followed by the assembly path/target framework/architecture.

data.name: Error
data.comment: Label for the count of assemblies that failed without producing a failed test (crash / non-zero exit / handshake failure) in the run summary, e.g. 'error: 1'.

data.name: DiscoveredTestsInAssembly
data.comment: Per-assembly discovery header printed by the dotnet test orchestrator; followed by the assembly path/target framework/architecture. {0} is the discovered test count.

data.name: DiscoveredTestsSummarySingular
data.comment: dotnet test orchestrator discovery total for a single assembly. {0} is the discovered test count.

data.name: DiscoveredTestsSummary
data.comment: dotnet test orchestrator discovery total across multiple assemblies. {0} is the discovered test count, {1} the assembly count.

data.name: CodeCoverageSummary
data.comment: Heading printed before the per-scope code coverage percentages in the run summary.

data.name: CoverageThresholdResults
data.comment: Heading printed before the coverage threshold pass/fail comparisons in the run summary.

data.name: CoverageThresholdPassed
data.comment: Coverage threshold comparison shown when the threshold is met. {0} is the actual coverage percentage, {1} is the threshold percentage.

data.name: CoverageThresholdFailed
data.comment: Coverage threshold comparison shown when the threshold is not met. {0} is the actual coverage percentage, {1} is the threshold percentage.

data.name: CoverageThresholdNoDataFailed
data.comment: Coverage threshold result shown when a scope has no coverable data and the configured policy treats that as a failure.

data.name: CoverageThresholdNoDataPassed
data.comment: Coverage threshold result shown when a scope has no coverable data and the configured policy treats that as a pass.

data.name: CoverageScopeOverall
data.comment: Label for the whole-run (overall) scope in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Line: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageScopeModule
data.comment: Label for the module scope level in coverage threshold aggregation descriptions.

data.name: CoverageScopeAssembly
data.comment: Label for the assembly scope level in coverage threshold aggregation descriptions.

data.name: CoverageScopeNamespace
data.comment: Label for the namespace scope level in coverage threshold aggregation descriptions.

data.name: CoverageScopeType
data.comment: Label for the type scope level in coverage threshold aggregation descriptions.

data.name: CoverageScopeFile
data.comment: Label for the file scope level in coverage threshold aggregation descriptions.

data.name: CoverageNoData
data.comment: Placeholder shown instead of a percentage when a coverage metric has no coverable data.

data.name: CoverageMetricLine
data.comment: Label for line code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Line: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricStatement
data.comment: Label for statement code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Statement: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricBranch
data.comment: Label for branch code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Branch: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricMethod
data.comment: Label for method code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Method: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricWithProducer
data.comment: Disambiguates duplicate coverage metric rows. {0} is the metric label and {1} is the coverage producer id.

data.name: CoverageMetricFunction
data.comment: Label for function code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Function: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricBlock
data.comment: Label for block code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Block: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricInstruction
data.comment: Label for instruction code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Instruction: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricRegion
data.comment: Label for region code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Region: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricClass
data.comment: Label for class code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Class: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricCondition
data.comment: Label for condition code coverage in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Condition: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageMetricComplexity
data.comment: Label for cyclomatic complexity in the coverage summary (e.g. 'Total - Complexity: 95.0%').

data.name: CoverageAggregationTotal
data.comment: Label for the total (aggregate) statistic used in a coverage threshold comparison (e.g. 'Line (Total): ...').

data.name: CoverageAggregationMinimum
data.comment: Label for the minimum-across-scopes statistic used in a coverage threshold comparison (e.g. 'Line (Minimum): ...').

data.name: CoverageAggregationAverage
data.comment: Label for the average-across-scopes statistic used in a coverage threshold comparison (e.g. 'Line (Average): ...').

data.name: CoverageAggregationMaximum
data.comment: Label for the maximum-across-scopes statistic used in a coverage threshold comparison (e.g. 'Line (Maximum): ...').

data.name: CoverageAggregationOver
data.comment: Coverage threshold aggregation description. {0} is the aggregation label and {1} is the population scope level.
